title: PlausiDen — docs
description: Install Forge. Author typed CMS. Ship in 60 seconds.

sections.title: Ship a typed site in 60 seconds.
sections.lede: Forge is one cargo install away. From zero to deployed in four commands.

sections.heading: From zero to deployed
sections.lede: Four commands. No accounts. No clouds. Just types.

items.title: Author
items.body: Drop one JSON file per page in cms/. Editor autocomplete via the $schema reference; closed-enum CmsSection variants enforce shape at parse time.

items.title: Build
items.body: forge build runs 27 audit phases + emits a Merkle-chained Ed25519-signed report. Output: static/ + reports/.

items.title: Deploy
items.body: rsync static/ to any host. Caddy, nginx, IPFS, Tor hidden service, your laptop. No platform lock-in.

sections.title: Author once. Render every variant.
sections.lede: Closed-enum CmsSection variants render through one render_page() function. 170 primitives compose every layout shape you've seen.

sections.heading: Reference
sections.lede: The complete documentation lives in the source tree.

items.title: 170 section kinds
items.body: Closed-enum CmsSection variants across Structural, Editorial, Marketing, Media, Commerce, Social, Forms, Navigation, Feedback, Game-Forum-Video, Auth, Crucible.

items.title: 14 themes
items.body: AMOLED true-black, WCAG-AA high-contrast pair, sepia, warm, ocean, forest, violet, rose. Picked via data-theme attribute or in-page toggle.

items.title: 27 audit phases
items.body: Configured in forge.toml. Tokens / CSP / a11y landmarks / theme contrast / phantom-button / unbuilt-route / external-assets / and more.

items.question: Where's the schema?
answer: cms-schema.json at your project root. Reference it via "$schema": "../cms-schema.json" in every cms/*.json. Editors with jsonls (VS Code / Zed / Helix / Neovim) provide inline autocomplete + validation.

items.question: How do I add a new primitive?
answer: Loom-side concern: extend CmsSection enum + render_section arm + skin.css block. See the PlausiDen-Loom contribution guide for the pattern.

items.question: Can I author with the GUI?
answer: loom edit serve ships a typed-CMS editor that writes back to cms/*.json. Or hand-edit the JSON — both paths are first-class.

items.question: How do I host on Tor?
answer: Add a DeployAdapter entry to your deploy.toml with kind = "onion". forge deploy then publishes your build to the hidden service.

sections.title: Open everything.
sections.lede: Forge, Loom, Crawler, Annotator, CMS are all open source. Every commit signed.
